mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) above its long-term trend (index > 100) signals robust manufacturing activity, which translates to stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO is above 100 but SPX produces negative returns in >60% of subsequent 20-day periods, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate to below 4.5% indicates a tight labor market that historically precedes wage inflation and Federal Reserve tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >50% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5%, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, real interest rates become negative, incentivizing investment in equities as a hedge against inflation, producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPI is rising and Fed funds is low but SPX returns are negative in >60% of 20-day windows, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1000K units signal construction sector health and consumer confidence, but this effect is amplified in a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 1%) as lower borrowing costs boost housing demand further, leading to positive forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 1000 and FEDFUNDS < 1% but SPX 5-day returns are negative in >60% of cases, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals recessionary conditions as employers reduce headcount, leading to lower consumer spending and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases by more than 0.2% in one month
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines by >0.2% but SPX 60-day returns are positive in >50% of instances, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Retail and food services sales (RSAFS) below 430,000 million dollars indicate a sharp contraction in consumer spending, which directly reduces corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS < 430000 but SPX 20-day returns are positive in >60% of instances, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.25%, it signals the end of an accommodative policy cycle and the beginning of monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 0.25% but SPX 60-day returns are positive in >50% of instances, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) level above 240 indicates high inflation that erodes purchasing power and raises uncertainty, leading to short-term negative S&P 500 returns as markets price in tighter monetary policy, over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL > 240 but SPX 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances, hypothesis fails.
